Tumor microenvironment and its spatial organization are a key area of study

Our challenge: improving high-grade serous ovarian cancer (HGSC) treatments

~50% of HGSC are Homologous Recombination-proficient (HRP)

Highly metastatic disease 
Poor prognosis 
(5y. OS ~32%)

Diverse molecular profiles

Immunotherapies have shown low efficacy

NACT

NACT

There is a pressing need to explore 
alternative therapeutic options for 
HRP patients

Matthews HK, Bertoli C, de Bruin RAM. Cell cycle control in cancer. Nat Rev Mol Cell Biol. 2022 Jan;23(1):74-88. doi: 10.1038/s41580-021-00404-3.

Distinct spatial cell cycle niches can be observed across tumors

Follow-up questions

Are these niches enriched in specific patient subtypes? 
Do they correlate with any of the clinical parameters (e.g. PFS, OS)
Do these neighborhoods display distinct immune cells?

‹#›

Take-home messages

Highly multiplexed imaging provides valuable spatial data of the TME

Obtaining high-quality data implies facing many technical challenges

We should closely inspect our images, “Seeing is believing”

Cell cycle and anti-tumor immunity are interrelated processes

We validated a t-CycIF antibody panel to identify cell cycle stages spatially

tCycIF reveals distinct tumoral cell cycle profiles and spatial niches, whose neighborhoods and clinical implications are yet to be explored

PIXELS
